channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Former law enforcement officers, military vets step up to save stranded hurricane survivors without FEMA
item.description: A group of civilians has banded together to help with disaster relief in North Carolina in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, which devastated several areas of the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security cost-of-living benefits increase announcement coming Thursday
item.description: Tens of millions of Social Security recipients will learn Thursday how big a cost-of-living boost they’ll get to their benefits next year.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden condemns ‘un-American’ 'lies' about federal storm response as Hurricane Milton nears Florida
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Wednesday blasted his predecessor for spreading an “onslaught of lies” about how the federal government is handling the damage from Hurricane Helene as another hurricane, Milton, was on the verge of making landfall in Florida.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A federal judge will hear more evidence on whether to reopen voter registration in Georgia
item.description: At least for now, a federal judge won’t order the state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: This is FEMA's role in preparing for Hurricane Milton
item.description: Milton is set to become the second hurricane to strike Florida in two weeks, and the Federal Emergency Management Agency is once again positioning supplies and personnel where they’re needed and coordinating with state and local officials.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance’s Midwestern roots appeal to Michigan swing-county voters despite Buckeye ties
item.description: Ohio Sen. JD Vance can help former President Donald Trump win Michigan thanks to his Midwestern upbringing, which will appeal to voters in the critical swing state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The disconnect between economic data and voters' concerns
item.description: Are you worried about money? Concerned about the economy? At 9 a.m. on Thursday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests talk about the financial issues affecting how people plan to vote.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Cari Dwyer, MPR News

item.title: Colorado Supreme Court dismisses lawsuit against Christian baker who refused to bake trans cake
item.description: The Colorado Supreme Court dismissed another lawsuit against Masterpiece Cakeshop's Jack Phillips, who faced accusations of discrimination for refusing to bake a custom cake.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court appears split on Oklahoma death row inmate’s fate
item.description: The Supreme Court appeared split over an Oklahoma death row inmate’s latest bid for freedom during arguments Wednesday, leaving his fate uncertain. Richard Glossip was convicted and sentenced to death for the 1997 murder of his former boss, motel owner Barry Van Treese.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has 9-point lead on most important issue to voters: poll
item.description: A majority of voters say the economy is the most important issue going into the presidential election, and former President Trump holds a 9-point lead over Vice President Kamala Harris on the topic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota, dies at 77
item.description: Former Sen. Tim Johnson, a centrist who was the last Democrat to hold statewide office in South Dakota and who spent nearly three decades in Congress, has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Human Rights Campaign launching ad blitz for Harris, other Democrats
item.description: Human Rights Campaign (HRC) and its Equality Votes political action committee are launching a multimillion-dollar digital ad blitz aimed at reaching "equality voters" for the Harris-Walz ticket in key battleground states, the group announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Military board substantiates misconduct but declines to fire Marine who adopted Afghan orphan
item.description: CAMP LEJEUNE, N.C. (AP) — A U.S. Marine whose adoption of an Afghan war orphan has spurred a yearslong legal battle and raised alarms at the highest levels of government will remain on active duty. A three-member panel of Marines said Tuesday that while Maj.
item.dc:creator: JULIET LINDERMAN, CLAIRE GALOFARO and MARTHA MENDOZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the U.S.
item.description: Nasir Ahmad Tawhedi, 27, of Oklahoma City told investigators after his arrest Monday that he had planned his attack to coincide with Election Day next month and that he and a co-conspirator expected to die as martyrs, according to charging documents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How Hurricane Helene may affect the election in North Carolina
item.description: None of the 13 affected counties in North Carolina have a fully operational elections board, according to the state's Secretary of State office.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's shifting abortion stance raises questions for evangelical support
item.description: Former President Trump’s softening stance on reproductive health has raised questions about his standing with white evangelical voters, a key part of his coalition dating back to 2016.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: How is Mayor Adams connected to the latest federal indictment?
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- In charging Mohamed Bahi with witness tampering and destroying records, federal authorities have shed light on the separate case against Mayor Eric Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: When was the last time New York voted red?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - New York has been a blue state since the U.S. began labeling states red or blue back in 2000 (more on that later).
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: It’s a close race for Michigan. These factors could decide it for Harris or Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris needs to win back Michigan voters President Joe Biden looked poised to lose. Donald Trump’s appeal remains strong here, but he’s not without his own struggles.
item.dc:creator: Majd Al-Waheidi, MPR News

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: The “Fox News Sunday” interview reflected a broader media blitz by presidential nominee Kamala Harris and Walz as the Democrats seek to garner public attention in the final 30 days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden says small business disaster loan program will soon run out of money
item.description: President Joe Biden says Congress needs to shore up a small business disaster loans fund, and fast. Meanwhile, the response to Hurricane Helene has become a presidential election issue.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: County official accuses two supervisors of preventing his promotion in filed claim
item.description: A former Registrar of Voters filed a claim against the county of San Diego, specifically making allegations against two sitting members on the Board of Supervisors.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5
